0: A wide-angle photorealistic shot of the same aged traditional Chinese study-bedroom, with a simple wooden bed, folded bedding, cracked plaster walls, tall wooden windows, hanging paper scrolls, an old wall map, dark wooden cabinets, a worn wooden writing desk, books, a desk lamp, and a wicker chair. The camera begins a slow 360-degree room scan by turning to the right while slightly pulling backward for the first five seconds, keeping the furniture layout stable and the room geometry coherent. The view remains inside the same small old room, with soft natural daylight, muted beige walls, warm dark wood, dusty floorboards, and no modern objects.
81: The camera continues turning right inside the same old Chinese study-bedroom after the initial backward pull. The view moves beyond the writing desk and begins revealing the wall that was hidden outside the first frame, keeping the old desk, lamp, books, wicker chair, bed, cabinets, windows, hanging scrolls, and old map spatially consistent. The room remains a single continuous small interior with cracked plaster, warm dark wood, dusty floorboards, soft daylight, no people, no modern objects, and no readable new text.
161: The camera now faces the hidden wall near the writing desk. This newly revealed wall is a weathered cracked plaster wall with an old single-leaf wooden door located close to the desk, worn wooden trim, aged corners, floor-wall junctions, and softened daylight falling across the surface. The door wall is the main focus, while the surrounding desk, wicker chair, windows, bed, cabinets, hanging scrolls, and old map remain part of the same coherent room. No hallway, no modern door, no extra furniture, and no readable new writing appear.
241: A steady continuation of the same 360-degree interior scan, showing another side of the aged room with cracked plaster, old wooden window frames, worn wall edges, dusty floorboards, and simple vintage wooden details. The camera keeps rotating smoothly to the right from a nearly fixed central position, preserving the scale and layout of the bed, desk, cabinets, door wall, windows, and wall decorations. The room remains quiet, still, photorealistic, and timeworn, with no modern objects and no new readable writing.
321: The camera continues the rightward 360-degree scan across the remaining side of the room, maintaining the same old study-bedroom layout and soft natural lighting. Weathered plaster surfaces, worn wooden trim, dusty floorboards, tall old window frames, and muted vintage textures remain consistent. The previously revealed old wooden door, the writing desk area, bed, cabinets, scrolls, and map stay connected within one compact room, with no people, no modern items, and no invented readable text.
401: The camera enters the final autoregressive block of the slow rightward 360-degree scan and gradually returns toward the original view of the old study-bedroom. The familiar bed, folded bedding, old map, hanging scrolls, dark wooden cabinets, writing desk, wicker chair, tall wooden windows, cracked plaster walls, and dusty floor come back into view with consistent geometry and lighting. The scene remains the same continuous room, quiet and unoccupied, with a vintage old-photo atmosphere and no modern elements.
